# Controlled Chapter: Before The Bell

The inn had stopped serving wine before dusk. Two guards remained beside the barred door, listening to the magistrate read names in the room beyond.

The clock struck once. The younger guard reached for the latch, but his captain caught his wrist and shook his head.

It was not rain, not wind, not footsteps. No one opened the door, raised a head, or spoke. The lamp watched like an eye; the table sank like a well; the window held the color of frozen water.

Then the bell rang from inside the locked room. The captain released the younger man's wrist. A line of red water appeared under the door, and every name on the magistrate's list became a death sentence.
